artists.name: Blink-182
artists.description: Pop-punk pioneers. Travis Barker drumming engine. Tom + Mark dual vocal tag team. Spans classic Enema era through 2023 reunion album.

artists.name: Green Day
artists.description: Three chords, maximum impact. Rob Cavallo production. American Idiot punk opera. Billie Joe Armstrong — rock's most efficient songwriter.

artists.name: Rage Against the Machine
artists.description: Tom Morello guitar-as-turntable. Zack de la Rocha rap-scream hybrid. No click tracks. Slow heavy grooves. Political fury as art form.

artists.name: Taylor Swift / Antonoff (Male Era)
artists.description: Male-perspective reframe of 14 Antonoff-produced Swift songs. Plus per-album prompts for 1989 through Midnights. Juno-6 synth DNA throughout.

artists.name: Russell Dickerson
artists.description: Modern country-pop romantic. Writes almost exclusively about his wife Kailey. R&B rhythms under bright Nashville production. Famous Back Home 2025 era.

artists.name: Thomas Rhett
artists.description: Genre-blending country-pop-R&B. Autobiographical storytelling. From doo-wop-country fusion to slow piano ballads. Hyper-specific lyric details.

artists.name: Country — Genre Study 2018–2025
artists.description: Top 10 hits per year 2018–2025. DNA analysis of what made each era's country hits. Universal style + lyric generation prompts.

artists.name: Pop Punk — Genre Study 2018–2025
artists.description: Pop punk revival era. Machine Gun Kelly through Olivia Rodrigo through Blink-182 reunion. Gen Z therapy-speak punk.

artists.name: Progressive Rock — Genre Study 2018–2025
artists.description: Porcupine Tree, Steven Wilson, Haken, Leprous era. Psychological alienation meets 7/8 time signatures. Modern prog DNA.

artists.name: Alternative Rock — Genre Study 2018–2025
artists.description: Glass Animals to Zach Bryan to Djo. TikTok resurrection factor. Hyper-specific personal imagery. The authentic loner aesthetic won.
